title: Unnecessary mathematical comparisons should not be made
description: Unnecessary mathematical comparisons should not be madeAnalyze your codeintentionality-logicalmaintainabilityCode SmellsuspiciousWhy is this an issue?More InfoCertainmathematical comparisonswill always return the same value, and should not be performed.Specifically, the following comparisons will return either alwaystrueor alwaysfalsedepending on the kind of comparison:comparing acharwith a numeric constant that is outside of the range ofcharcomparing afloatwith a numeric constant that is outside of the range offloatcomparing alongwith a numeric constant that is outside of the range oflongcomparing aulongwith a numeric constant that is outside of the range ofulongetc.Noncompliant code examplefloat f = 42.0f; if (f <= double.MaxValue) { } // Noncompliant: always true if (f > double.MaxValue
recommendation: Review and fix according to the rule guidelines.

logic.detection_conditions: Find comparisons of numeric types with out-of-range constants

conditions: Type compared to value outside its range

logic.reasoning: Comparing a type to a constant outside its range always gives the same result, making the comparison useless.
